title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Browser and Node Client Systems · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: A partner SDK team integrating an unfamiliar third-party endpoint must satisfy changed endpoint, payload, authority, byte, and time constraints without a recipe for header fields, media types, negotiation, json, and runtime validation, producing representation negotiation and validation boundary. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch representation negotiation and validation boundary with automated and judgment evidence. Learner…
objectives: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use Content-Type for enclosed content and Accept for preferred response media ranges with explicit fallback. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Field Semantics
steps.instruction:
lab case — partner TypeScript SDK asks for application/json and receives Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Encoding gzip, Vary Accept-Language, and field revision:5. Distinguish metadata layers, bound decoded bytes, and reject a changed value type.
